While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:8.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

exec_args.sql:
WITH book_years AS (
  SELECT 
    book_id,
    CAST(SUBSTRING(details FROM '(19[0-9]{2}|20[0-2][0-9])') AS INTEGER) as year,
    CONCAT(CAST(FLOOR(CAST(SUBSTRING(details FROM '(19[0-9]{2}|20[0-2][0-9])') AS INTEGER) / 10) * 10 AS TEXT), 's') as decade
  FROM books_info 
  WHERE details ~ '(19[0-9]{2}|20[0-2][0-9])'
),
joined_reviews AS (
  SELECT 
    by.decade,
    r.purchase_id,
    CAST(r.rating AS FLOAT) as rating,
    SUBSTRING(r.purchase_id FROM 'purchaseid_([0-9]+)') as review_num_id,
    SUBSTRING(by.book_id FROM 'bookid_([0-9]+)') as book_num_id
  FROM review r
  JOIN book_years by ON SUBSTRING(r.purchase_id FROM 'purchaseid_([0-9]+)') = SUBSTRING(by.book_id FROM 'bookid_([0-9]+)')
)
SELECT 
  decade,
  COUNT(DISTINCT book_num_id) as num_books,
  COUNT(*) as num_ratings,
  ROUND(AVG(rating), 3) as avg_rating
FROM joined_reviews
GROUP BY decade
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT book_num_id) >= 10
ORDER BY avg_rating DESC;

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:8.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

import json
import re
import os
from collections import defaultdict

# Load the books data (we have the file path from the previous query)
books_file = var_functions.query_db_76

# We still need to query the review database to get all reviews

# For now, let's just examine the books data file
print('Books file path:', books_file)
print('File exists:', os.path.exists(books_file) if isinstance(books_file, str) else 'Not a path')

# Read the books data
if isinstance(books_file, str) and os.path.exists(books_file):
    with open(books_file, 'r') as f:
        books = json.load(f)
    print('Loaded {} books'.format(len(books)))
    print('Sample books:')
    for i, book in enumerate(books[:5]):
        print('  {}. book_id={}, year={}'.format(i+1, book['book_id'], book['year']))
else:
    print('Books data is not a file path, it might be inline data')
    print('Type:', type(books_file))
    if isinstance(books_file, list):
        books = books_file
        print('Loaded {} books from inline data'.format(len(books)))

args.query:
SELECT 
  book_id,
  CASE 
    WHEN details ~ '19[0-9]{2}' THEN CONCAT(CAST(FLOOR(CAST(SUBSTRING(details FROM '19[0-9]{2}') AS INTEGER) / 10) * 10 AS TEXT), 's')
    WHEN details ~ '20[0-9]{2}' THEN CONCAT(CAST(FLOOR(CAST(SUBSTRING(details FROM '20[0-9]{2}') AS INTEGER) / 10) * 10 AS TEXT), 's')
    ELSE NULL
  END as decade
FROM books_info 
WHERE details ~ '(19[0-9]{2}|20[0-9]{2})'

    ELSE NULL

args.code:
import json
import os
from collections import defaultdict

# Load the books decades data
books_file = var_functions.query_db_82

# Load the reviews data
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db_84

# Check if these are file paths or data lists
if isinstance(books_file, str) and os.path.exists(books_file):
    with open(books_file, 'r') as f:
        books_decades = json.load(f)
else:
    books_decades = books_file

if isinstance(reviews_file, str) and os.path.exists(reviews_file):
    with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
else:
    reviews = reviews_file

print('Loaded {} books with decades and {} reviews'.format(len(books_decades), len(reviews)))

# Create mapping from numeric book ID to decade
book_decade_map = {}
for book in books_decades:
    # Extract numeric ID from bookid_X
    num_id = book['book_id'].split('_')[1]
    book_decade_map[num_id] = book['decade']

print('Books in decade map: {}'.format(len(book_decade_map)))

# Join reviews with books
joined_reviews = []
for review in reviews:
    # Extract numeric ID from purchaseid_X
    review_num_id = review['purchase_id'].split('_')[1]
    if review_num_id in book_decade_map:
        joined_reviews.append({
            'book_id': review_num_id,
            'decade': book_decade_map[review_num_id],
            'rating': float(review['rating'])
        })

print('Successfully joined {} reviews to books'.format(len(joined_reviews)))

# Group by decade and calculate statistics
decade_groups = defaultdict(lambda: {'ratings': [], 'books': set()})

for review in joined_reviews:
    decade_groups[review['decade']]['ratings'].append(review['rating'])
    decade_groups[review['decade']]['books'].add(review['book_id'])

print('Found {} different decades'.format(len(decade_groups)))

# Calculate average ratings and filter by at least 10 distinct books
decade_results = []
for decade, data in decade_groups.items():
    num_books = len(data['books'])
    if num_books >= 10:
        avg_rating = sum(data['ratings']) / len(data['ratings'])
        decade_results.append({
            'decade': decade,
            'avg_rating': avg_rating,
            'num_books': num_books,
            'num_ratings': len(data['ratings'])
        })

# Sort by average rating descending
decade_results.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

print('Decades with at least 10 books: {}'.format(len(decade_results)))
for i, result in enumerate(decade_results, 1):
    print('{}. {}: avg_rating={}, num_books={}, num_ratings={}'.format(
        i, result['decade'], round(result['avg_rating'], 3), 
        result['num_books'], result['num_ratings']
    ))

# Get the best decade
if decade_results:
    best_decade = decade_results[0]['decade']
    best_avg = decade_results[0]['avg_rating']
    print('\\nBEST DECADE: {} with average rating {}'.format(best_decade, round(best_avg, 3)))
else:
    best_decade = None
    print('\\nNo decades found with at least 10 books')

result = {'best_decade': best_decade, 'decade_results': decade_results}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:8.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. val_args.code:
import json
import os
from collections import defaultdict

# Load the books decades data
books_file = var_functions.query_db_82

# Load the reviews data
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db_84

# Check if these are file paths or data lists
if isinstance(books_file, str) and os.path.exists(books_file):
    with open(books_file, 'r') as f:
        books_decades = json.load(f)
else:
    books_decades = books_file

if isinstance(reviews_file, str) and os.path.exists(reviews_file):
    with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
else:
    reviews = reviews_file

print('Loaded {} books with decades and {} reviews'.format(len(books_decades), len(reviews)))

# Create mapping from numeric book ID to decade
book_decade_map = {}
for book in books_decades:
    # Extract numeric ID from bookid_X
    num_id = book['book_id'].split('_')[1]
    book_decade_map[num_id] = book['decade']

print('Books in decade map: {}'.format(len(book_decade_map)))

# Join reviews with books
joined_reviews = []
for review in reviews:
    # Extract numeric ID from purchaseid_X
    review_num_id = review['purchase_id'].split('_')[1]
    if review_num_id in book_decade_map:
        joined_reviews.append({
            'book_id': review_num_id,
            'decade': book_decade_map[review_num_id],
            'rating': float(review['rating'])
        })

print('Successfully joined {} reviews to books'.format(len(joined_reviews)))

# Group by decade and calculate statistics
decade_groups = defaultdict(lambda: {'ratings': [], 'books': set()})

for review in joined_reviews:
    decade_groups[review['decade']]['ratings'].append(review['rating'])
    decade_groups[review['decade']]['books'].add(review['book_id'])

print('Found {} different decades'.format(len(decade_groups)))

# Calculate average ratings and filter by at least 10 distinct books
decade_results = []
for decade, data in decade_groups.items():
    num_books = len(data['books'])
    if num_books >= 10:
        avg_rating = sum(data['ratings']) / len(data['ratings'])
        decade_results.append({
            'decade': decade,
            'avg_rating': avg_rating,
            'num_books': num_books,
            'num_ratings': len(data['ratings'])
        })

# Sort by average rating descending
decade_results.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

print('Decades with at least 10 books: {}'.format(len(decade_results)))
for i, result in enumerate(decade_results, 1):
    print('{}. {}: avg_rating={}, num_books={}, num_ratings={}'.format(
        i, result['decade'], round(result['avg_rating'], 3), 
        result['num_books'], result['num_ratings']
    ))

# Get the best decade
if decade_results:
    best_decade = decade_results[0]['decade']
    best_avg = decade_results[0]['avg_rating']
    print('\\nBEST DECADE: {} with average rating {}'.format(best_decade, round(best_avg, 3)))
else:
    best_decade = None
    print('\\nNo decades found with at least 10 books')

result = {'best_decade': best_decade, 'decade_results': decade_results}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:8.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

reviews_file = var_functions.query_db_84

print('Reviews file:', reviews_file, type(reviews_file))

if isinstance(books_file, str) and os.path.exists(books_file):
    with open(books_file, 'r') as f:
        books_data = json.load(f)
elif isinstance(books_file, list):
    books_data = books_file
else:
    print('Books data not found')
    books_data = []

# Load the reviews data  
if isinstance(reviews_file, str) and os.path.exists(reviews_file):
    with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
        reviews_data = json.load(f)
elif isinstance(reviews_file, list):
    reviews_data = reviews_file
else:
    print('Reviews data not found')
    reviews_data = []

print('Loaded {} books and {} reviews'.format(len(books_data), len(reviews_data)))

# Show sample data
if books_data:
    print('Sample book:', books_data[0])
if reviews_data:
    print('Sample review:', reviews_data[0])

# Create mapping from numeric book ID to decade
book_decade_map = {}
for book in books_data:
    num_id = book['book_id'].split('_')[1]
    book_decade_map[num_id] = book['decade']

print('Books in map:', len(book_decade_map))

# Join with reviews
joined = []
for review in reviews_data:
    num_id = review['purchase_id'].split('_')[1]
    if num_id in book_decade_map:
        joined.append({
            'book_id': num_id,
            'decade': book_decade_map[num_id],
            'rating': float(review['rating'])
        })

print('Joined reviews:', len(joined))

# Group by decade
groups = defaultdict(lambda: {'ratings': [], 'books': set()})
for item in joined:
    groups[item['decade']]['ratings'].append(item['rating'])
    groups[item['decade']]['books'].add(item['book_id'])

print('Decades found:', len(groups))

# Calculate statistics
results = []
for decade, data in groups.items():
    num_books = len(data['books'])
    if num_books >= 10:
        avg_rating = sum(data['ratings']) / len(data['ratings'])
        results.append({
            'decade': decade,
            'avg_rating': avg_rating,
            'num_books': num_books,
            'num_ratings': len(data['ratings'])
        })

results.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

print('Decades with >=10 books:', len(results))
for i, r in enumerate(results):
    print(f"{i+1}. {r['decade']}: avg={round(r['avg_rating'], 3)}, books={r['num_books']}, ratings={r['num_ratings']}")

best_decade = results[0]['decade'] if results else None
print('Best decade:', best_decade)

output = {'best_decade': best_decade}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(output))

else:
    print('Reviews data not found')
    reviews_data = []

print('Loaded {} books and {} reviews'.format(len(books_data), len(reviews_data)))

# Show sample data
if books_data:
    print('Sample book:', books_data[0])
if reviews_data:
    print('Sample review:', reviews_data[0])

# Create mapping from numeric book ID to decade
book_decade_map = {}
for book in books_data:
    num_id = book['book_id'].split('_')[1]
    book_decade_map[num_id] = book['decade']

print('Books in map:', len(book_decade_map))

# Join with reviews
joined = []
for review in reviews_data:
    num_id = review['purchase_id'].split('_')[1]
    if num_id in book_decade_map:
        joined.append({
            'book_id': num_id,
            'decade': book_decade_map[num_id],
            'rating': float(review['rating'])
        })

print('Joined reviews:', len(joined))

# Group by decade
groups = defaultdict(lambda: {'ratings': [], 'books': set()})
for item in joined:
    groups[item['decade']]['ratings'].append(item['rating'])
    groups[item['decade']]['books'].add(item['book_id'])

print('Decades found:', len(groups))

# Calculate statistics
results = []
for decade, data in groups.items():
    num_books = len(data['books'])
    if num_books >= 10:
        avg_rating = sum(data['ratings']) / len(data['ratings'])
        results.append({
            'decade': decade,
            'avg_rating': avg_rating,
            'num_books': num_books,
            'num_ratings': len(data['ratings'])
        })

results.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

print('Decades with >=10 books:', len(results))
for i, r in enumerate(results):
    print(f"{i+1}. {r['decade']}: avg={round(r['avg_rating'], 3)}, books={r['num_books']}, ratings={r['num_ratings']}")

best_decade = results[0]['decade'] if results else None
print('Best decade:', best_decade)

output = {'best_decade': best_decade}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(output))

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

args.query:
WITH book_decades AS (
  SELECT 
    book_id,
    SUBSTRING(details FROM '19[0-9]{2}|20[0-9]{2}') as year_str
  FROM books_info 
  WHERE details ~ '(19[0-9]{2}|20[0-9]{2})'
)
SELECT 
  book_id,
  CONCAT(CAST(FLOOR(CAST(year_str AS INTEGER) / 10) * 10 AS TEXT), 's') as decade
FROM book_decades;

FROM book_decades;

args.code:
import json, os, re
from collections import defaultdict

# Get the data from the query results
print('Checking available variables...')
all_vars = list(globals().keys())
query_vars = [v for v in all_vars if 'query_db_' in v]
print('Query result variables:', query_vars)

# Load books data - it should be in var_functions.query_db_92
books_var = 'var_functions.query_db_92'
if books_var in globals():
    books_data = globals()[books_var]
    # If it's a file path, load it
    if isinstance(books_data, str) and os.path.exists(books_data):
        with open(books_data, 'r') as f:
            books = json.load(f)
    else:
        books = books_data
    print('Books loaded:', len(books))
else:
    print('Books data not found')
    books = []

# Load reviews data - it should be in var_functions.query_db_84
reviews_var = 'var_functions.query_db_84'
if reviews_var in globals():
    reviews_data = globals()[reviews_var]
    # If it's a file path, load it
    if isinstance(reviews_data, str) and os.path.exists(reviews_data):
        with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
            reviews = json.load(f)
    else:
        reviews = reviews_data
    print('Reviews loaded:', len(reviews))
else:
    print('Reviews data not found')
    reviews = []

print('\nProcessing...')
# Create mapping from numeric book ID to decade
book_decade_map = {}
for book in books:
    if isinstance(book, dict) and 'book_id' in book and 'decade' in book:
        num_id = book['book_id'].split('_')[1]
        book_decade_map[num_id] = book['decade']
    else:
        print('Invalid book record:', book)

print('Books in map:', len(book_decade_map))

# Join reviews with books
joined = []
for review in reviews:
    if isinstance(review, dict) and 'purchase_id' in review and 'rating' in review:
        num_id = review['purchase_id'].split('_')[1]
        if num_id in book_decade_map:
            joined.append({
                'book_id': num_id,
                'decade': book_decade_map[num_id],
                'rating': float(review['rating'])
            })
    else:
        print('Invalid review record:', review)

print('Reviews joined:', len(joined))

# Group by decade
groups = defaultdict(lambda: {'ratings': [], 'books': set()})
for item in joined:
    groups[item['decade']]['ratings'].append(item['rating'])
    groups[item['decade']]['books'].add(item['book_id'])

print('Decades found:', len(groups))

# Calculate statistics
results = []
for decade, data in groups.items():
    num_books = len(data['books'])
    if num_books >= 10:
        avg_rating = sum(data['ratings']) / len(data['ratings'])
        avg_rounded = round(avg_rating, 3)
        results.append({
            'decade': decade,
            'avg_rating': avg_rounded,
            'num_books': num_books,
            'num_ratings': len(data['ratings'])
        })

# Sort by average rating descending
results.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:8.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

val_args.code:
import json, os, re
from collections import defaultdict

# Get the data from the query results
print('Checking available variables...')
all_vars = list(globals().keys())
query_vars = [v for v in all_vars if 'query_db_' in v]
print('Query result variables:', query_vars)

# Load books data - it should be in var_functions.query_db_92
books_var = 'var_functions.query_db_92'
if books_var in globals():
    books_data = globals()[books_var]
    # If it's a file path, load it
    if isinstance(books_data, str) and os.path.exists(books_data):
        with open(books_data, 'r') as f:
            books = json.load(f)
    else:
        books = books_data
    print('Books loaded:', len(books))
else:
    print('Books data not found')
    books = []

# Load reviews data - it should be in var_functions.query_db_84
reviews_var = 'var_functions.query_db_84'
if reviews_var in globals():
    reviews_data = globals()[reviews_var]
    # If it's a file path, load it
    if isinstance(reviews_data, str) and os.path.exists(reviews_data):
        with open(reviews_data, 'r') as f:
            reviews = json.load(f)
    else:
        reviews = reviews_data
    print('Reviews loaded:', len(reviews))
else:
    print('Reviews data not found')
    reviews = []

print('\nProcessing...')
# Create mapping from numeric book ID to decade
book_decade_map = {}
for book in books:
    if isinstance(book, dict) and 'book_id' in book and 'decade' in book:
        num_id = book['book_id'].split('_')[1]
        book_decade_map[num_id] = book['decade']
    else:
        print('Invalid book record:', book)

print('Books in map:', len(book_decade_map))

# Join reviews with books
joined = []
for review in reviews:
    if isinstance(review, dict) and 'purchase_id' in review and 'rating' in review:
        num_id = review['purchase_id'].split('_')[1]
        if num_id in book_decade_map:
            joined.append({
                'book_id': num_id,
                'decade': book_decade_map[num_id],
                'rating': float(review['rating'])
            })
    else:
        print('Invalid review record:', review)

print('Reviews joined:', len(joined))

# Group by decade
groups = defaultdict(lambda: {'ratings': [], 'books': set()})
for item in joined:
    groups[item['decade']]['ratings'].append(item['rating'])
    groups[item['decade']]['books'].add(item['book_id'])

print('Decades found:', len(groups))

# Calculate statistics
results = []
for decade, data in groups.items():
    num_books = len(data['books'])
    if num_books >= 10:
        avg_rating = sum(data['ratings']) / len(data['ratings'])
        avg_rounded = round(avg_rating, 3)
        results.append({
            'decade': decade,
            'avg_rating': avg_rounded,
            'num_books': num_books,
            'num_ratings': len(data['ratings'])
        })

# Sort by average rating descending
results.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

print('\nDecades with at least 10 books:', len(results))
for i, r in enumerate(results):
    print(f"{i+1}. {r['decade']}: avg={r['avg_rating']}, books={r['num_books']}, ratings={r['num_ratings']}")

best_decade = results[0]['decade'] if results else None
print('\nBEST DECADE:', best_decade)

output = {'best_decade': best_decade, 'all_results': results}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(output))

from collections import defaultdict

print('Starting analysis...')

# Load books data from the stored query result

reviews_file = globals()['var_functions.query_db_84']

print('Got file paths')

# Load the data
with open(books_file, 'r') as f:
    books = json.load(f)

with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

print('Loaded {} books and {} reviews'.format(len(books), len(reviews)))

# Create mapping from numeric book ID to decade
book_decade_map = {}
for book in books:
    num_id = book['book_id'].split('_')[1]
    book_decade_map[num_id] = book['decade']

print('Books in decade map: {}'.format(len(book_decade_map)))

# Join reviews with books
joined = []
for review in reviews:
    num_id = review['purchase_id'].split('_')[1]
    if num_id in book_decade_map:
        joined.append({
            'book_id': num_id,
            'decade': book_decade_map[num_id],
            'rating': float(review['rating'])
        })

print('Joined {} reviews to books'.format(len(joined)))

# Group by decade
groups = defaultdict(lambda: {'ratings': [], 'books': set()})
for item in joined:
    groups[item['decade']]['ratings'].append(item['rating'])
    groups[item['decade']]['books'].add(item['book_id'])

print('Found {} decades'.format(len(groups)))

# Calculate averages and filter by >=10 books
results = []
for decade, data in groups.items():
    num_books = len(data['books'])
    if num_books >= 10:
        avg_rating = sum(data['ratings']) / len(data['ratings'])
        results.append({
            'decade': decade,
            'avg_rating': round(avg_rating, 3),
            'num_books': num_books,
            'num_ratings': len(data['ratings'])
        })

results.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

print('Decades with >=10 books: {}'.format(len(results)))
for i, r in enumerate(results[:]):
    print('{}. {} avg={} books={} ratings={}'.format(
        i+1, r['decade'], r['avg_rating'], r['num_books'], r['num_ratings']
    ))

best_decade = results[0]['decade'] if results else None
print('Best decade: {}'.format(best_decade))

# Return result
output = {'best_decade': best_decade}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(output))

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:8.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!

val_args.code:
import json
import os
from collections import defaultdict

print('Starting analysis...')

# Load books data from the stored query result
books_file = globals()['var_functions.query_db_92']
reviews_file = globals()['var_functions.query_db_84']

print('Got file paths')

# Load the data
with open(books_file, 'r') as f:
    books = json.load(f)

with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

print('Loaded {} books and {} reviews'.format(len(books), len(reviews)))

# Create mapping from numeric book ID to decade
book_decade_map = {}
for book in books:
    num_id = book['book_id'].split('_')[1]
    book_decade_map[num_id] = book['decade']

print('Books in decade map: {}'.format(len(book_decade_map)))

# Join reviews with books
joined = []
for review in reviews:
    num_id = review['purchase_id'].split('_')[1]
    if num_id in book_decade_map:
        joined.append({
            'book_id': num_id,
            'decade': book_decade_map[num_id],
            'rating': float(review['rating'])
        })

print('Joined {} reviews to books'.format(len(joined)))

# Group by decade
groups = defaultdict(lambda: {'ratings': [], 'books': set()})
for item in joined:
    groups[item['decade']]['ratings'].append(item['rating'])
    groups[item['decade']]['books'].add(item['book_id'])

print('Found {} decades'.format(len(groups)))

# Calculate averages and filter by >=10 books
results = []
for decade, data in groups.items():
    num_books = len(data['books'])
    if num_books >= 10:
        avg_rating = sum(data['ratings']) / len(data['ratings'])
        results.append({
            'decade': decade,
            'avg_rating': round(avg_rating, 3),
            'num_books': num_books,
            'num_ratings': len(data['ratings'])
        })

results.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

print('Decades with >=10 books: {}'.format(len(results)))
for i, r in enumerate(results[:]):
    print('{}. {} avg={} books={} ratings={}'.format(
        i+1, r['decade'], r['avg_rating'], r['num_books'], r['num_ratings']
    ))

best_decade = results[0]['decade'] if results else None
print('Best decade: {}'.format(best_decade))

# Return result
output = {'best_decade': best_decade}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(output))

args.query:
SELECT 
  book_id,
  CONCAT(CAST(FLOOR(CAST(SUBSTRING(details FROM '19[0-9]{2}|20[0-9]{2}') AS INTEGER) / 10) * 10 AS TEXT), 's') as decade,
  SUBSTRING(book_id FROM 'bookid_([0-9]+)') as num_id
FROM books_info 
WHERE details ~ '(19[0-9]{2}|20[0-9]{2})'

args.code:
import json
import os
from collections import defaultdict

# Load the books data (from query_db_92)
books_file = globals()['var_functions.query_db:98']
reviews_file = globals()['var_functions.query_db:84']

print('Books data file:', books_file, type(books_file))
print('Reviews data file:', reviews_file, type(reviews_file))

# Load JSON data
with open(books_file, 'r') as f:
    books = json.load(f)

with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews = json.load(f)

print('Loaded {} books and {} reviews'.format(len(books), len(reviews)))

# Create mapping from numeric ID to decade
id_to_decade = {}
for book in books:
    id_to_decade[book['num_id']] = book['decade']

print('Mapping size: {}'.format(len(id_to_decade)))

# Join reviews with books
decade_ratings = defaultdict(lambda: {'ratings': [], 'books': set()})

matched = 0
for review in reviews:
    # Extract numeric ID from purchase_id
    review_id = review['purchase_id'].replace('purchaseid_', '')
    if review_id in id_to_decade:
        decade = id_to_decade[review_id]
        decade_ratings[decade]['ratings'].append(float(review['rating']))
        decade_ratings[decade]['books'].add(review_id)
        matched += 1

print('Matched {} reviews'.format(matched))
print('Decades with data: {}'.format(len(decade_ratings)))

# Calculate statistics for each decade
results = []
for decade, data in decade_ratings.items():
    num_books = len(data['books'])
    if num_books >= 10:
        avg_rating = sum(data['ratings']) / len(data['ratings'])
        results.append({
            'decade': decade,
            'avg_rating': round(avg_rating, 3),
            'num_books': num_books,
            'num_ratings': len(data['ratings'])
        })

# Sort by average rating descending
results.sort(key=lambda x: x['avg_rating'], reverse=True)

print('Decades with >=10 books: {}'.format(len(results)))
for i, r in enumerate(results[:10]):
    print('{}. {}: avg={}, books={}, ratings={}'.format(
        i+1, r['decade'], r['avg_rating'], r['num_books'], r['num_ratings']
    ))

best_decade = results[0]['decade'] if results else None
best_avg = results[0]['avg_rating'] if results else 0

print('BEST DECADE: {} with average rating {}'.format(best_decade, best_avg))

output = {'best_decade': best_decade}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(output))

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: Four Stars
var_functions.query_db:8.text: Not as developed as Stephanie but I like the characters so far.

var_functions.query_db:8.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:8.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!
